steps.title: Test the communication claim
steps.content: The second team claims the sounds are primarily for communication with members of the same species. A finding that removes or weakens the presence of such an audience would most directly challenge that claim.

steps.title: Separate alternative explanations
steps.content: Choice A connects sound production to simulated attacks, not to communication. Choice B concerns species that did not produce sound in the attacks. Choice D weakens a predator-defense explanation but does not rule out intraspecies communication. A solitary, isolated sound-producing caterpillar is least compatible with communication to others. The answer is C.
